title: Forging press temperature and hydraulic pressure operating envelope
content: For forging press operation, high hydraulic pressure combined with high temperature may indicate overload, oil degradation, die misalignment, or cooling insufficiency. Recommendations must not bypass safety interlocks.
recommended_actions: Check hydraulic pressure trend, relief valve status and oil temperature.
Inspect cooling system and die lubrication condition.
If pressure and temperature both exceed warning limits, request operator confirmation before continuing production.
safety_notes: Do not directly modify pressure parameters without human approval.

title: FMEA cases for impact-like vibration in forging equipment
content: High crest factor or high impulsive vibration can be associated with bearing defect, guide rail wear, die looseness, eccentric loading, or mechanical impact during forging stroke.
recommended_actions: Inspect bearing, guide rail clearance and die fastening condition.
Compare vibration waveform across multiple strokes to exclude transient process impact.
Increase sampling frequency if impulsive events are aliased.
safety_notes: If abnormal impact increases rapidly, stop feeding and request manual inspection.

title: Historical case: unknown fault under changed billet and speed condition
content: When billet size and stroke speed changed together, a previous closed-set diagnostic model misclassified an unknown coupling fault as a known bearing fault. Multi-model comparison and manual review reduced maintenance risk.
recommended_actions: Use an unknown-aware model and compare with a process-rule model.
Trigger human review if unknown score remains high after digital-twin validation.
Add the confirmed case to the knowledge base and update the model profile.
safety_notes: Unknown faults should not be handled by automatic parameter change.

title: Algorithm profile note for model orchestration
content: For low-risk closed-set tasks, one reliable low-latency model can be used. For open-set, partial label or high-risk tasks, complementary models should be called and output consistency should be checked.
recommended_actions: Use multi-model consistency checking for high-risk or unknown-risk scenarios.
Attach model version, confidence, unknown score and knowledge source to every maintenance suggestion.
safety_notes: The agent must not directly generate a diagnosis without invoking the model pool.
